cases.title: Wilbourne v. Pacific Presbytery
cases.synopsis: Pacific Presbytery's judicial commission read a prejudicial six-page report prior to TE Wilbourne's BCO 38-1 confession of guilt, then deposed him; the SJC sustained the complaint, annulled the deposition, and recommended treating his 17-month effective suspension as definite suspension from office. Complaints 2020-08 and 2020-09 by fellow TEs and the Session of Pacific Crossroads Church were sustained by reference to this decision.

cases.title: Gendall, Hightower, and Lien v. Pacific Presbytery

parties.complainant_or_appellant: TE Shawn Gendall, TE Russell Hightower, and TE Timothy Lien

cases.synopsis: Three Teaching Elders complained against Pacific Presbytery's handling of TE Wilbourne's BCO 38-1 confession and subsequent deposition. The SJC sustained the complaints by reference to its decision in Case 2020-07.

cases.title: Ozbalt and Barr v. Pacific Presbytery

parties.complainant_or_appellant: RE Sean Ozbalt and RE Erin Barr

topics: standing of Session members as complainants
cases.synopsis: Session members of Pacific Crossroads Church filed a complaint against Pacific Presbytery's handling of TE Wilbourne's deposition. The SJC sustained the complaint by reference to its decision in Case 2020-07.

cases.title: Gordon v. Southern New England Presbytery
cases.synopsis: Mr. Gordon was excommunicated by a PCA Session after his former OPC congregation joined the PCA against his expressed wishes; he contended the PCA Session lacked jurisdiction over him. The SJC majority denied his appeal affirming the excommunication, while a four-member dissent argued the SJC should have ruled the case out of order for lack of jurisdiction and standing, as Gordon never voluntarily became a PCA member.

cases.title: Myers v. Illiana Presbytery
cases.synopsis: TE Aaron Myers appealed his conviction on charges of maltreatment of his wife and fits of anger by Illiana Presbytery; the SJC denied the appeal and upheld all four specifications of error arguments raised by the appellant, affirming the indefinite suspension from office and suspension from the sacraments.

cases.title: Michelson v. Northwest Georgia Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC ruled Case 2021-01 administratively out of order as prematurely filed because Presbytery had not yet completed its hearing on the complaint; the complainant's time to elevate was reset.

cases.title: Tippins v. Northwest Georgia Presbytery
cases.synopsis: On a BCO 40-5 request, the SJC advised that the matter should be referred to the General Assembly's Review of Presbytery Records Committee, with three members dissenting.

cases.title: Eagle v. Savannah River Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC ruled Case 2020-10 administratively out of order as prematurely filed because Presbytery had not yet completed its hearing on the complaint; the complainant's time to elevate was reset.

cases.title: Eudaly and Light v. Southwest Florida Presbytery

parties.complainant_or_appellant: TE Dustyn Eudaly and TE Steven Light

cases.synopsis: The SJC ruled Case 2021-05 administratively out of order because the complainants had affiliated with another branch of the visible church on February 9, 2020, losing PCA jurisdiction and therefore lacking standing to file the complaint.

cases.title: In re Korean Southwest Orange County (Citation)
cases.synopsis: The SJC cited Korean Southwest Orange County Presbytery to appear at the March 2022 Stated Meeting unless it provided satisfactory written responses to part (d) of the RPR Report from the 2021 General Assembly regarding exceptions to its records.

cases.title: Speck v. Missouri Presbytery
cases.synopsis: TE Ryan Speck complained that Missouri Presbytery erred in declining to find a strong presumption of guilt against TE Greg Johnson regarding four allegations arising from his involvement in and teaching at the Revoice 2018 conference; the SJC sustained the complaint in part on Issue 1 (particularly regarding Theological Judgments 2, 3, and 5 about terminology, gay identity, and spiritual friendship), denied Issues 2 and 3, and remanded the matter to Missouri Presbytery for a new hearing on specific questions.
